description: Golden evaluation dataset covering the full legal corpus: Constitution of India, Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita 2023 (BNS), Bharatiya Nagarik Suraksha Sanhita 2023 (BNSS), and Bharatiya Sakshya Adhiniyam 2023 (BSA). Category A answers are verified against the source documents. Category B questions are intentionally out of scope.

categories.A: Answerable from the corpus - system must answer with citations
categories.B: Out of scope - system must refuse

qa_pairs.question: What does Article 21 say about the right to life and personal liberty?
qa_pairs.expected_answer: Article 21 states that no person shall be deprived of his life or personal liberty except according to procedure established by law.

qa_pairs.question: What does Article 14 of the Constitution guarantee?
qa_pairs.expected_answer: Article 14 guarantees that the State shall not deny to any person equality before the law or the equal protection of the laws within the territory of India.

qa_pairs.question: What does Article 19 of the Constitution guarantee?
qa_pairs.expected_answer: Article 19 guarantees to all citizens freedom of speech and expression, the right to assemble peaceably and without arms, the right to form associations or unions, the right to move freely throughout India, the right to reside and settle in any part of India, and the right to practise any profession or carry on any occupation, trade or business.

qa_pairs.question: What remedy does Article 32 provide for enforcement of fundamental rights?
qa_pairs.expected_answer: Article 32 guarantees the right to move the Supreme Court by appropriate proceedings for enforcement of fundamental rights. The Supreme Court has power to issue directions, orders or writs including habeas corpus, mandamus, prohibition, quo warranto and certiorari.

qa_pairs.question: What is the procedure for amendment of the Constitution under Article 368?
qa_pairs.expected_answer: Under Article 368, an amendment may be initiated by a Bill in either House of Parliament. The Bill must be passed in each House by a majority of the total membership and by not less than two-thirds of members present and voting. The Bill is then presented to the President who gives assent. For amendments to certain specified provisions, ratification by Legislatures of not less than one-half of the States is also required.

qa_pairs.question: What does the Preamble of the Constitution of India declare?
qa_pairs.expected_answer: The Preamble declares India to be a Sovereign Socialist Secular Democratic Republic. It resolves to secure to all citizens justice, liberty, equality, and to promote fraternity assuring the dignity of the individual and the unity and integrity of the Nation.

qa_pairs.question: What are the qualifications required to be elected as President of India?
qa_pairs.expected_answer: Under Article 58, a person must be a citizen of India, have completed thirty-five years of age, and be qualified for election as a member of the House of the People. A person holding any office of profit under any Government is not eligible.

qa_pairs.question: What does Article 356 provide regarding President's Rule in a State?
qa_pairs.expected_answer: Article 356 provides that if the President is satisfied that the government of a State cannot be carried on in accordance with the Constitution, the President may assume to himself all or any of the functions of the Government of the State and powers vested in the Governor.

qa_pairs.question: What does Article 44 provide regarding a Uniform Civil Code?
qa_pairs.expected_answer: Article 44 provides that the State shall endeavour to secure for the citizens a uniform civil code throughout the territory of India. This is a Directive Principle of State Policy in Part IV of the Constitution.

qa_pairs.question: What are the Fundamental Duties listed under Article 51A?
qa_pairs.expected_answer: Article 51A lists duties of every citizen including abiding by the Constitution, cherishing ideals of the national struggle, upholding sovereignty and integrity of India, defending the country, promoting harmony and common brotherhood, protecting the natural environment, developing scientific temper, safeguarding public property, and striving towards excellence.

qa_pairs.question: What does Article 22 protect citizens against regarding arrest and detention?
qa_pairs.expected_answer: Article 22 provides that no person who is arrested shall be detained without being informed of the grounds of arrest. Every arrested person has the right to consult a legal practitioner of their choice and shall be produced before the nearest Magistrate within twenty-four hours.

qa_pairs.question: What does Article 352 deal with regarding National Emergency?
qa_pairs.expected_answer: Article 352 provides that if the President is satisfied that a grave emergency exists whereby the security of India or any part is threatened by war, external aggression or armed rebellion, he may by Proclamation declare a National Emergency. Such a proclamation must be approved by both Houses of Parliament.

qa_pairs.question: What does Article 13 provide regarding laws inconsistent with fundamental rights?
qa_pairs.expected_answer: Article 13 provides that all laws in force before commencement of the Constitution that are inconsistent with Part III shall be void to the extent of such inconsistency. The State shall not make any law which takes away or abridges the rights conferred by Part III and any such law shall be void.

qa_pairs.question: What does Article 17 of the Constitution deal with?
qa_pairs.expected_answer: Article 17 abolishes untouchability and forbids its practice in any form. The enforcement of any disability arising out of untouchability shall be an offence punishable in accordance with law.

qa_pairs.question: What does Article 21A provide regarding the right to education?
qa_pairs.expected_answer: Article 21A provides that the State shall provide free and compulsory education to all children of the age of six to fourteen years in such manner as the State may by law determine.

qa_pairs.question: What is the composition of the Rajya Sabha?
qa_pairs.expected_answer: Under Article 80, the Rajya Sabha shall consist of not more than two hundred and thirty-eight representatives of States and Union territories, and twelve members nominated by the President having special knowledge or practical experience in literature, science, art and social service.

qa_pairs.question: What does Article 124 provide regarding the establishment of the Supreme Court?
qa_pairs.expected_answer: Article 124 provides for the establishment of the Supreme Court of India consisting of a Chief Justice of India and such number of other Judges as Parliament may prescribe. Every Judge shall be appointed by the President after consultation with such Judges of the Supreme Court and High Courts as the President deems necessary.

qa_pairs.question: What powers does Article 226 confer on High Courts?
qa_pairs.expected_answer: Article 226 empowers every High Court to issue to any person or authority, including the Government, directions, orders or writs including habeas corpus, mandamus, prohibition, quo warranto and certiorari for the enforcement of fundamental rights and for any other purpose.

qa_pairs.question: What does Article 300A provide regarding the right to property?
qa_pairs.expected_answer: Article 300A provides that no person shall be deprived of his property save by authority of law.

qa_pairs.question: What does Article 12 define as the State for purposes of fundamental rights?
qa_pairs.expected_answer: Article 12 defines the State as including the Government and Parliament of India, the Government and Legislature of each State, and all local or other authorities within the territory of India or under the control of the Government of India.

qa_pairs.question: What are the types of punishments to which offenders are liable under the Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita?
qa_pairs.expected_answer: Under the BNS, the punishments to which offenders are liable are: death, imprisonment for life, imprisonment which may be rigorous or simple, forfeiture of property, fine, and community service.

qa_pairs.question: What is the definition of culpable homicide under the Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita?
qa_pairs.expected_answer: Under the BNS, culpable homicide is defined as causing death by doing an act with the intention of causing death, or with the intention of causing such bodily injury as is likely to cause death, or with the knowledge that the act is likely to cause death.

qa_pairs.question: What is the punishment for murder under the Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita?
qa_pairs.expected_answer: Under the BNS, whoever commits murder shall be punished with death or imprisonment for life, and shall also be liable to fine.

qa_pairs.question: What constitutes theft under the Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita?
qa_pairs.expected_answer: Under the BNS, theft involves dishonestly taking any moveable property out of the possession of any person without that person's consent. The moving of the property in order to such taking is called theft.

qa_pairs.question: What is the definition of robbery under the Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita?
qa_pairs.expected_answer: Under the BNS, in all robbery there is either theft or extortion. Theft becomes robbery when the offender voluntarily causes or attempts to cause death, hurt or wrongful restraint, or fear of instant death, hurt or wrongful restraint in order to commit theft, carry away property or prevent resistance. Extortion becomes robbery when it is committed in the presence of the victim causing fear of instant death, hurt or wrongful restraint.

qa_pairs.question: What is the definition of dacoity under the Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita?
qa_pairs.expected_answer: Under the BNS, when five or more persons conjointly commit or attempt to commit a robbery, or where the whole number of persons conjointly committing or attempting to commit a robbery, and persons present and aiding such commission or attempt, amount to five or more, every person so committing, attempting or aiding is said to commit dacoity.

qa_pairs.question: What constitutes cheating under the Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita?
qa_pairs.expected_answer: Under the BNS, whoever by deceiving any person, fraudulently or dishonestly induces the person so deceived to deliver any property, or to consent that any person shall retain any property, or intentionally induces the person so deceived to do or omit to do anything which he would not do or omit if he were not so deceived, and which act or omission causes or is likely to cause damage or harm to that person in body, mind, reputation or property, is said to cheat.

qa_pairs.question: What does the Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita say about abetment of an offence?
qa_pairs.expected_answer: Under the BNS, a person abets the doing of a thing who instigates any person to do that thing, or engages with one or more other persons in any conspiracy for the doing of that thing, or intentionally aids by any act or illegal omission the doing of that thing. Whoever abets an offence shall be punished with the punishment provided for the offence if the act is committed in consequence of the abetment.

qa_pairs.question: What does the Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita say about criminal conspiracy?
qa_pairs.expected_answer: Under the BNS, when two or more persons agree to do or cause to be done an illegal act or an act which is not illegal by illegal means, such an agreement is designated a criminal conspiracy. A person party to a criminal conspiracy to commit an offence shall be punished in the same manner as if he had abetted such offence.

qa_pairs.question: What does the Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita say about the right of private defence of the body?
qa_pairs.expected_answer: Under the BNS, every person has the right to defend his own body and the body of any other person against any offence affecting the human body. The right of private defence in no case extends to the inflicting of more harm than it is necessary to inflict for the purpose of defence.

qa_pairs.question: What is the punishment for attempt to commit murder under the Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita?
qa_pairs.expected_answer: Under the BNS, whoever does any act with such intention or knowledge, and under such circumstances, that if he thereby caused death he would be guilty of murder, shall be punished with imprisonment for life or rigorous imprisonment for a term which may extend to ten years, and shall also be liable to fine.

qa_pairs.question: What constitutes kidnapping under the Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita?
qa_pairs.expected_answer: Under the BNS, kidnapping from India means conveying any person beyond the limits of India without the consent of that person or of some person legally authorised to consent on his behalf. Kidnapping from lawful guardianship means taking or enticing any minor or person of unsound mind out of the keeping of their lawful guardian without the guardian's consent.

qa_pairs.question: What does the Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita say about hurt and grievous hurt?
qa_pairs.expected_answer: Under the BNS, whoever causes bodily pain, disease or infirmity to any person is said to cause hurt. Grievous hurt includes emasculation, permanent loss of sight, loss of hearing, loss of a limb, destruction of a joint, permanent disfiguration of the head or face, fracture of bone or tooth, and any hurt which endangers life or causes severe bodily pain for twenty days.

qa_pairs.question: What is community service as a punishment under the Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita?
qa_pairs.expected_answer: Community service is one of the punishments prescribed under the BNS. It is work ordered by a court as a punishment for offenders instead of imprisonment, benefiting the community. The BNS introduced community service as a new form of punishment for certain minor offences.

qa_pairs.question: What does the Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita say about general exceptions to criminal liability?
qa_pairs.expected_answer: The BNS provides general exceptions to criminal liability including acts done by a person of unsound mind, acts done by a child under seven years of age, acts done under intoxication where the person was incapable of knowing the nature of the act, acts done in good faith, and acts done by consent of the person harmed.

qa_pairs.question: What is the procedure for registering a First Information Report under the Bharatiya Nagarik Suraksha Sanhita?
qa_pairs.expected_answer: Under the BNSS, every information relating to a cognizable offence must be reduced to writing by the officer in charge of the police station or entered in a book maintained by that officer. The informant shall sign it and the substance shall be entered in a book. A copy of the FIR shall be given to the informant free of charge.

qa_pairs.question: What is the concept of Zero FIR under the Bharatiya Nagarik Suraksha Sanhita?
qa_pairs.expected_answer: Under the BNSS, an officer in charge of a police station must register an FIR regardless of the area where the offence was committed. Such an FIR, called a Zero FIR, shall be transferred to the police station having jurisdiction. This ensures that an informant is not turned away due to jurisdictional issues.

qa_pairs.question: Under what circumstances can a police officer arrest a person without a warrant under the Bharatiya Nagarik Suraksha Sanhita?
qa_pairs.expected_answer: Under the BNSS, a police officer may arrest without a warrant any person who commits a cognizable offence in his presence, who has been concerned in a cognizable offence or against whom a reasonable complaint or credible information exists, who has been proclaimed as an offender, who is found in possession of house-breaking implements, who has been released on licence and violates its conditions, or who is required to provide security.

qa_pairs.question: What are the rights of an arrested person under the Bharatiya Nagarik Suraksha Sanhita?
qa_pairs.expected_answer: Under the BNSS, every arrested person has the right to be informed of the grounds of arrest, the right to be informed of bail entitlement for bailable offences, the right to have a friend, relative or nominated person informed of the arrest, the right to meet an advocate during interrogation, and the right to be produced before a magistrate within twenty-four hours of arrest.

qa_pairs.question: What is the maximum period for which an accused can be detained in police custody under the Bharatiya Nagarik Suraksha Sanhita?
qa_pairs.expected_answer: Under the BNSS, the Magistrate may authorise the detention of an accused in police custody for a term not exceeding fifteen days in total. For offences punishable with death, imprisonment for life or imprisonment for a term of not less than ten years, the initial period of custody may be extended up to sixty days in total.

qa_pairs.question: What does the Bharatiya Nagarik Suraksha Sanhita say about bail for bailable offences?
qa_pairs.expected_answer: Under the BNSS, when any person accused of a bailable offence is arrested or detained without warrant, the officer in charge of the police station or the court shall release that person on bail. The person is entitled to bail as a matter of right for bailable offences.

qa_pairs.question: What are the provisions for anticipatory bail under the Bharatiya Nagarik Suraksha Sanhita?
qa_pairs.expected_answer: Under the BNSS, any person who apprehends arrest on an accusation of having committed a non-bailable offence may apply to the High Court or the Court of Session for a direction under this section that in the event of such arrest he shall be released on bail.

qa_pairs.question: What does the Bharatiya Nagarik Suraksha Sanhita say about default bail?
qa_pairs.expected_answer: Under the BNSS, if a charge-sheet is not filed within the prescribed period — sixty days for offences punishable with death, life imprisonment or imprisonment for ten or more years, and sixty days for other offences — the accused shall be released on bail on an application made, unless the court directs otherwise for reasons recorded in writing.

qa_pairs.question: What is the procedure for a summary trial under the Bharatiya Nagarik Suraksha Sanhita?
qa_pairs.expected_answer: Under the BNSS, in every summary trial the Magistrate shall record the substance of the evidence and a judgment containing a brief statement of the reasons for the finding. Summary trials are conducted for petty offences and for offences punishable with imprisonment for a term not exceeding three years.

qa_pairs.question: What does the Bharatiya Nagarik Suraksha Sanhita say about the medical examination of an arrested person?
qa_pairs.expected_answer: Under the BNSS, when a person is arrested, he shall be examined by a medical officer in the service of the Central or State Government. The arrested person or their nominated person is entitled to receive a copy of the medical examination report. For female arrested persons, the examination shall be conducted by a female medical officer.

qa_pairs.question: What does the Bharatiya Nagarik Suraksha Sanhita say about the examination of witnesses by police?
qa_pairs.expected_answer: Under the BNSS, a police officer making an investigation may examine orally any person supposed to be acquainted with the facts and circumstances of the case. Such a person is bound to answer all questions relating to the case, except questions the answers to which would have a tendency to expose him to criminal charge or penalty.

qa_pairs.question: What does the Bharatiya Nagarik Suraksha Sanhita provide regarding electronic communication of FIR?
qa_pairs.expected_answer: Under the BNSS, a person may report a cognizable offence by electronic communication. The information may be recorded through electronic means and a copy shall be sent to the concerned Magistrate. This provision allows registration of FIRs through digital platforms.

qa_pairs.question: What does the Bharatiya Nagarik Suraksha Sanhita say about bail for non-bailable offences?
qa_pairs.expected_answer: Under the BNSS, when any person accused of a non-bailable offence is arrested, the court may release him on bail. However, bail shall not be granted if there appear reasonable grounds for believing that the person has been guilty of an offence punishable with death or imprisonment for life.

qa_pairs.question: What does the Bharatiya Nagarik Suraksha Sanhita say about the trial of warrant cases by magistrates?
qa_pairs.expected_answer: Under the BNSS, a warrant case is a case relating to an offence punishable with death, imprisonment for life or imprisonment for a term exceeding two years. In trials before magistrates of warrant cases instituted on a police report, the magistrate shall, when the accused appears, hear the prosecution and take the evidence.

qa_pairs.question: What does the Bharatiya Nagarik Suraksha Sanhita say about proclamation for a person absconding?
qa_pairs.expected_answer: Under the BNSS, if a court has reason to believe that any person against whom a warrant has been issued has absconded, the court may publish a written proclamation requiring the person to appear at a specified time and place. If the person fails to appear, the court may attach the property of the person.

qa_pairs.question: What does the Bharatiya Sakshya Adhiniyam say about the admissibility of electronic records as evidence?
qa_pairs.expected_answer: Under the BSA, electronic records are admissible as evidence. A document or statement contained in an electronic record which is printed on paper, stored, recorded or copied in optical or magnetic media produced by a computer shall be deemed to be a document and admissible as evidence if conditions relating to the computer and its output are satisfied.

qa_pairs.question: What does the Bharatiya Sakshya Adhiniyam say about the burden of proof?
qa_pairs.expected_answer: Under the BSA, whoever desires any court to give judgment as to any legal right or liability dependent on the existence of facts which he asserts must prove the existence of those facts. The burden of proof in a suit or proceeding lies on that person who would fail if no evidence at all were given on either side.

qa_pairs.question: What does the Bharatiya Sakshya Adhiniyam say about confessions made to police officers?
qa_pairs.expected_answer: Under the BSA, no confession made to a police officer shall be proved as against a person accused of any offence. A confession made by an accused while in the custody of a police officer shall not be proved against him unless it is made in the immediate presence of a Magistrate.

qa_pairs.question: What does the Bharatiya Sakshya Adhiniyam say about dying declarations?
qa_pairs.expected_answer: Under the BSA, statements made by a person as to the cause of his death, or as to any of the circumstances of the transaction which resulted in his death, in cases in which the cause of that person's death comes in question, are relevant whether the person was or was not at the time of the statement under expectation of death.

qa_pairs.question: What constitutes an admission under the Bharatiya Sakshya Adhiniyam?
qa_pairs.expected_answer: Under the BSA, an admission is a statement, oral or documentary or contained in electronic form, which suggests any inference as to any fact in issue or relevant fact, and which is made by any of the persons and under the circumstances listed in the provisions regarding admissions.

qa_pairs.question: What does the Bharatiya Sakshya Adhiniyam say about expert opinion?
qa_pairs.expected_answer: Under the BSA, when the court has to form an opinion upon a point of foreign law, science, art, identity of handwriting or finger impressions, the opinions of persons specially skilled in such subjects are relevant facts. Such persons are called experts.

qa_pairs.question: What does the Bharatiya Sakshya Adhiniyam say about relevancy of facts?
qa_pairs.expected_answer: Under the BSA, facts which are the occasion, cause, or effect — immediate or otherwise — of relevant facts or facts in issue, or which constitute the state of things under which they happened, or which afforded an opportunity for their occurrence, are relevant.
qa_pairs.source_hint: BSA, Chapter on Relevancy of Facts

qa_pairs.question: What does the Bharatiya Sakshya Adhiniyam say about secondary evidence?
qa_pairs.expected_answer: Under the BSA, secondary evidence includes copies of documents, counterparts of documents, and oral accounts of the contents of documents. Secondary evidence may be given of the existence, condition or contents of a document in circumstances like when the original is with the opposite party who fails to produce it.

qa_pairs.question: What does the Bharatiya Sakshya Adhiniyam say about the examination of witnesses?
qa_pairs.expected_answer: Under the BSA, witnesses shall first be examined in chief, then cross-examined, and then re-examined. A party may, with the leave of the court, recall any witness who has been examined. The court may also put questions to any witness for the purpose of discovering or obtaining proper proof of relevant facts.
qa_pairs.source_hint: BSA, Chapter on Examination of Witnesses

qa_pairs.question: What does the Bharatiya Sakshya Adhiniyam say about the presumption as to electronic records?
qa_pairs.expected_answer: Under the BSA, the court shall presume that every electronic signature and electronic record is genuine and has not been tampered with unless the contrary is proved. The court may also presume that an electronic message forwarded by the originator was sent by him.

qa_pairs.question: What are the GST rates applicable to food items in India?
qa_pairs.expected_answer: NOT APPLICABLE - out of scope. GST rates are governed by GST Acts and GST Council decisions, not the documents in this corpus.

qa_pairs.question: What is the minimum wage for workers in India?
qa_pairs.expected_answer: NOT APPLICABLE - out of scope. Minimum wages are governed by the Minimum Wages Act, not the documents in this corpus.
qa_pairs.source_hint: Minimum Wages Act - not in corpus

qa_pairs.question: What are the provisions of the Hindu Marriage Act 1955 regarding divorce?
qa_pairs.expected_answer: NOT APPLICABLE - out of scope. The Hindu Marriage Act 1955 is not part of the corpus.
qa_pairs.source_hint: Hindu Marriage Act 1955 - not in corpus

qa_pairs.question: What is the penalty for insider trading under SEBI regulations?
qa_pairs.expected_answer: NOT APPLICABLE - out of scope. SEBI regulations are not part of the corpus.

qa_pairs.question: What are the income tax slabs applicable for individuals in India for 2024-25?
qa_pairs.expected_answer: NOT APPLICABLE - out of scope. Income tax slabs are governed by the Finance Act and Income Tax Act, not the documents in this corpus.
qa_pairs.source_hint: Income Tax Act - not in corpus

qa_pairs.question: What are the provisions of the Companies Act 2013 regarding board meetings?
qa_pairs.expected_answer: NOT APPLICABLE - out of scope. The Companies Act 2013 is not part of the corpus.

qa_pairs.question: What are the time limits for providing information under the Right to Information Act 2005?
qa_pairs.expected_answer: NOT APPLICABLE - out of scope. The RTI Act 2005 is not part of the corpus.

qa_pairs.question: What are the eligibility criteria for applying for a passport in India?
qa_pairs.expected_answer: NOT APPLICABLE - out of scope. Passport eligibility is governed by the Passports Act 1967, not the documents in this corpus.

qa_pairs.question: What are the provisions of the Consumer Protection Act 2019 regarding filing a complaint?
qa_pairs.expected_answer: NOT APPLICABLE - out of scope. The Consumer Protection Act 2019 is not part of the corpus.
qa_pairs.source_hint: Consumer Protection Act 2019 - not in corpus

qa_pairs.question: What are the provisions of the Factories Act 1948 regarding working hours?
qa_pairs.expected_answer: NOT APPLICABLE - out of scope. The Factories Act 1948 is not part of the corpus.
